theme: a vein network that survives a cut
title: Leaf Vein Remodeling: A Network That Survives a Cut
innovation_hook: Fluctuating/multi-load driving of the same remodeling rule is what preserves protective loops that a single steady load would prune away.
strategies: do-nothing baseline: gamma=0 keeps every vein at unit conductance (the uniform mesh, the checker's own baseline)

aggregate-only reinforcement: drive the remodeling rule with only the all-sinks-at-once load and a moderate exponent -- efficient for that one load, but prunes veins the individual sinks needed and leaves a fragile trunk

fluctuating multi-load reinforcement: drive the SAME rule with the per-sink (and grouped) loads so every sink gets to reinforce its own path at some point, preserving redundant loops that survive any single cut
exponent + schedule co-tuning: search over both the loading weights and the reinforcement exponent to trade aggregate transport efficiency against worst-cut per-sink resilience
